input.task: I edited my workflow's .tsx and now `smithers up --resume` fails with 'Cannot resume run because durable metadata changed: workflow entry file changed'. How do I continue the run without losing the steps that already finished, and without hand-editing smithers.db?

verify.rubric: A correct answer must point to time-travel recovery — fork/replay from the last good snapshot/frame (smithers fork/replay/rewind) which preserves completed nodes — and must NOT recommend editing smithers.db by hand or implying --force (which starts fresh and loses work) is the right fix.

input.task: My run shows status `waiting-event`. How do I tell whether it is genuinely waiting on a <Signal>/<WaitForEvent> versus blocked after exhausting retries, and what is the recovery for each case?

verify.rubric: Must distinguish the two meanings and map each to the right recovery: deliver a signal (smithers signal) for a real wait, vs retry-task / fork / inspect for a failure-block. Mentioning `smithers why`/`inspect` to disambiguate is a plus.

input.task: Write a minimal Smithers workflow with ONE <Task> that produces its output deterministically in code — no agent, no LLM call — returning an object that matches its Zod output schema.

How do I make EVERY agent in a Smithers workflow use a specific model (say Fable)? I set the session model but the workflow's agents ignored it.

verify.rubric: Must state that agents do NOT inherit the session/CLI model — each agent's model is set explicitly where the agent is constructed (agents.ts / new XAgent({ model })), and the workflow must use those agents. Must not claim a global/session flag silently applies.

input.task: How do I block a destructive tool call (e.g. rm -rf) before it runs inside a Smithers agent? Is there a preToolUse/beforeTool interceptor in Smithers?

verify.rubric: Correct answer: Smithers has no per-tool preToolUse interceptor; gate at the workflow level (an <Approval>/<HumanTask>/<Signal> before the risky step, or allowTools restriction / --root containment) or via the underlying agent's hooks (Claude Code settings.json). Must not invent a Smithers preToolUse API.

My run reports status `completed` but most of its fan-out agents actually failed. How do I check whether the run truly succeeded, and what should I look at?

verify.rubric: Must explain inspecting per-node/agent outcomes (smithers inspect / node / events / scores) rather than trusting the top-level status, and acknowledge that a completed status can mask failed children. Bonus: bounded concurrency to avoid the rate-limit failures in the first place.

input.task: Does <Aspects> tokenBudget/costBudget/latencySlo get ENFORCED at runtime, or is it metadata only?

verify.rubric: Per the docs, <Aspects> propagates budget metadata to a subtree but runtime enforcement was historically NOT implemented (enforcement was added for tokens+latency in 0.24.1). A correct answer should reflect that nuance rather than asserting blanket enforcement. Accept either an explicitly-version-aware answer or 'check the version/docs'.

input.task: How do I start a Smithers workflow so it keeps running in the background while I'm away, and then monitor it to completion?

verify.rubric: Must use detached start (smithers up -d / --detach) and monitoring verbs (ps, inspect --watch, logs -f, why). Bonus: supervise for auto-resume of stale runs.

input.task: If I run `smithers init` in a project that already has .smithers/ workflows and tickets I edited, will it overwrite my changes? How can I see exactly what it wrote vs skipped?

verify.rubric: Must convey that init is non-destructive (skips existing files) and that --format json surfaces writtenFiles vs skippedFiles. Must not claim it clobbers edits.

I fan out 50 agents at once and hit rate limits. Which prop/component caps how many run concurrently so I don't burst?

I added a field to my workflow's typed input Zod schema and a new run fails with 'SQLiteError: table input has no column named X'. Why, and what's the clean fix?

verify.rubric: The input table is created from the Zod input schema; adding a field needs a fresh DB/run (or a migration) because the existing table lacks the column. Correct fixes: start a new run/DB (the schema is per-DB) or recreate the table — not abandoning typed input. Should recognize the cause (schema-derived table) rather than guessing.
